title: Plain Rice
description: A simple and fluffy basmati rice recipe that pairs perfectly with curries, dals, and gravies. Lightly scented with optional whole spices for a subtle aroma.

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: 1 tbsp ghee or oil (optional, for extra aroma and texture)

instruction.title: Step 1: Rinse the Rice
steps: Rinse the basmati rice under cold running water 3-4 times until the water runs clear.
This removes excess starch and prevents the rice from becoming sticky.

instruction.title: Step 2: Soak the Rice
steps: Soak the rinsed rice in enough water for 20-30 minutes.
This helps the grains expand and cook evenly.

instruction.title: Step 3: Drain
steps: After soaking, drain the rice thoroughly.
Heat the ghee/oil in a medium pot over medium heat.
Add the whole spices (optional) and sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant.

instruction.title: Step 4: Add Rice
steps: Add the drained rice to the pot.
Gently sauté the rice for 1-2 minutes, coating the grains with the ghee and spices.

instruction.title: Step 5: Add Water and Salt
steps: Pour in 2 cups of water and add salt.

instruction.title: Step 6: Cook the Rice
steps: Bring the water to a boil on high heat.
Once boiling, reduce heat to the lowest setting, cover the pot with a tight-fitting lid, and let it simmer for 12-15 minutes.
Avoid lifting the lid during this time.

instruction.title: Step 7: Rest
steps: After the cooking time, turn off the heat and let the rice rest, still covered, for 10 minutes.
This allows steam to finish cooking the rice and makes it fluffy.

instruction.title: Step 8: Fluff and Serve
steps: Remove the lid and gently fluff the rice with a fork before serving.

servingSuggestions.title: Serving Suggestions
items: Serve hot with dal, curry, or gravy.
Pairs well with chicken, mutton, paneer, or vegetable dishes.
Enjoy with pickle, papad, or yogurt for a simple meal.

chefTips: Use aged basmati rice for the best aroma and texture.
Avoid stirring too much during cooking to prevent breaking the grains.
Adjust water slightly if you prefer softer or firmer rice.
